name: Shelley Berman
bio: Shelley Berman was an American comedian who pioneered introspective, neurotic observational comedy performed sitting on a stool, often miming one-sided telephone conversations. His debut album Inside Shelley Berman won the first Grammy Award for a spoken comedy recording in 1959, and in 1961 he became the first stand-up comedian to headline Carnegie Hall.

timeline.ev: Joins Chicago improv troupe the Compass Players, precursor to Second City
Hired as a comedian at Mister Kellys in Chicago, signs with Verve Records
Inside Shelley Berman becomes a hit comedy album
First stand-up comedian to perform at Carnegie Hall
Begins recurring role as Larry Davids father on Curb Your Enthusiasm
Emmy nomination for Curb Your Enthusiasm

accolades: Won the first Grammy Award for Best Comedy Performance Spoken Word (1959)
First stand-up comedian to headline Carnegie Hall (1961)
Earned multiple gold records for his comedy albums
Primetime Emmy nomination for Curb Your Enthusiasm (2008)

machine.reason: zero clips on file — every comic needs at least 1 official clip
